message:
test(fcp-core): pin documented u64 wraparound policy across counters and chains (kvs22)

Pins three distinct overflow policies that already live in the codebase
so a future refactor cannot silently change them. The test file is the
canonical write-up of which sites use which policy and why.

Sites covered:

1. CRDT GCounter — `saturating_add` per-actor; `u128 saturating_add`
   in `value()`. An actor's per-actor count clamps at `u64::MAX`; the
   merged sum across actors clamps at `u128::MAX`. The reasoning is
   that a CRDT counter must remain monotone non-decreasing under merge,
   so panicking or wrapping would violate the lattice property.

2. Chain sequence numbers (`AuditEvent::follows`,
   `RevocationEvent::follows`, `CheckpointProposal::seq_follows_prev`)
   — `checked_add(1)`. When `prev.seq == u64::MAX`, the successor
   cannot legitimately follow; the chain refuses to advance instead of
   wrapping. This blocks an attacker who reaches u64::MAX from forging
   a "next" event by exploiting wrap.

3. capability.rs `expires_at + clock_skew_tolerance` —
   `saturating_add`. A wrapping add here would let a near-`u64::MAX`
   expiry silently flip "expired" → "valid" after wrap, which is a
   security-impacting failure mode. Saturation pins it expired-forever.

Test does NOT pin panic behaviour — none of these sites currently
panic on overflow, and forcing panic would block legitimate moves
between checked/saturating variants. The contract is "no wrap, no
silent overflow"; specific arithmetic flavour is implementation choice.

Also documents the rationale for the three policies in the module-level
doc comment so reviewers reading the test understand which kind of
arithmetic each subsystem owes its callers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
